tc.title: Transfer Certificates
tc.description: Manage Transfer Certificate requests, approvals, and issuance.

empty.noDataDescription: Requested transfer certificates will appear here.

requestDialog.title: Request Transfer Certificate
requestDialog.description: Start a new TC request for a student. Reason must be at least 10 characters.
requestDialog.studentSearchPlaceholder: Search by name or admission number…
requestDialog.reasonPlaceholder: Explain why the student is leaving (shown on TC)

fieldHelp.academicYearBody: The last academic session the student completed at your institute. This is printed on the TC and used to compute the 'Class studying in' field. Usually this is the current active year — pick a past year only if you are issuing a TC for a previously-withdrawn student whose record was reopened.
fieldHelp.reasonBody: Printed on the TC and visible to the receiving institute — be factual and neutral. Boards reject TCs with subjective or punitive wording ('ill-discipline', 'failed repeatedly') because they prejudice the student's next admission. Stick to objective facts: relocation, financial reasons, family circumstances, change of curriculum.
fieldHelp.reasonExample: Good: 'Parents have relocated to Pune due to job transfer.' 'Parents wish to shift child to a residential school.' Avoid: 'Poor academic performance', 'Disciplinary issues' — use the separate remarks field or suspension records instead.

rejectDialog.title: Reject Transfer Certificate
rejectDialog.description: Provide a reason for rejection. This is recorded in the audit log.
rejectDialog.reasonPlaceholder: Explain why this TC is being rejected

fieldHelp.reasonBody: Visible to the requester and the principal's reviewer log. Since a rejected TC blocks the student's next admission until resolved, be specific enough that the requester knows exactly what to fix — 'fee dues not cleared', 'library book not returned (Ref LIB-2891)', 'TC request needs Principal signature'. Vague rejections like 'Not eligible' cause repeat submissions and back-and-forth.

duplicateDialog.title: Request Duplicate TC
duplicateDialog.description: Provide a reason (FIR reference, affidavit details) for the duplicate.

fieldHelp.reasonBody: Duplicate TCs are watermarked 'Duplicate' and the serial number is suffixed with -D. Because the receiving institute will scrutinise duplicates, include the legal evidence: FIR number and date for lost originals, affidavit details for damaged originals, or court order references. Without these, boards may reject the duplicate outright.
fieldHelp.reasonExamples: Good: 'Original TC lost in transit. FIR #DEL/2025/048291 filed on 14 March 2025 at Hauz Khas PS. Affidavit by father attached as ANX-1.' 'Original TC destroyed in house fire. Loss certificate from fire brigade attached.'

other.title: Certificates
other.description: Bonafide, character, migration and other institute certificates.

empty.noDataDescription: Certificates of this type will appear here.

dialog.title: Issue {type} Certificate
dialog.description: Select the template, student, and purpose for this certificate.
dialog.studentSearchPlaceholder: Search by name or admission number…
dialog.previewDescription: This is how the certificate will look once issued.
dialog.previewMissing: Select a template and a student first

fieldHelp.templateBody: The pre-approved certificate template determines the letterhead, seal, signatures, and legal wording. Templates are set up by the super-admin and cannot be edited here — if the template looks wrong, ask them to publish a new version. The same template can be shared by multiple certificate types (e.g. one generic template for bonafide/character/migration).
fieldHelp.purposeBody: Why the student or parent is asking for this certificate. Printed on the certificate body (e.g. 'issued for the purpose of applying to …'). Being specific protects the institute — a certificate issued 'for all official purposes' is sometimes rejected by government offices, while 'for railway concession pass renewal' is accepted immediately.
fieldHelp.purposeExamples: Good: 'Scholarship application — National Means-cum-Merit', 'Railway concession pass renewal', 'Passport application', 'Change of surname after court decree #DEL/2025/4412'. Avoid 'official purpose' or 'personal use'.

empty.noMatchDescription: Try adjusting your filters or search terms.
